questions.q: この英文の内容として最も適切なものを1つ選びなさい。
questions.a: 言語が「死んで」しまった結果として母語話者や書き手が消えていき、ヒエログリフもそのようにして意味と音を失った。
questions.b: ヒエログリフの記号は、意味は何世紀もの間に失われたが、音（読み方）のほうは変わらず後世まで伝わり続けた。
questions.c: それを使う人がいなくなったために滅んだ言語があり、ヒエログリフもその一例で、記号の意味も音も長い年月の間に失われた。
questions.d: ヒエログリフは母語話者を失いはしたが、その記号の意味と音は現在も正確にわかったままである。
questions.explanation: 本文は「母語として話したり書いたりする人がいなくなったために言語が死んだ」と述べ、ヒエログリフをその例として挙げ、記号の意味と音の両方が何世紀もかけて失われたとしているので、Cが正解です。Aは原因と結果が逆（話者が消えたから死んだのであって、死んだから消えたのではない）、Bは音だけが失われたことにしていて「意味も音も失われた」と矛盾、Dは意味も音も失われたという本文と正反対です。

questions.q: Which one has the closest meaning to the sentences above?
questions.a: Some languages lost all their native speakers and writers because those languages had already died, and hieroglyphics disappeared in the very same way.
questions.b: Some languages died out once no one was left to speak or write them naturally, and hieroglyphics is one whose symbols' meanings and sounds were lost over many centuries.
questions.c: Hieroglyphics stopped being used, and although the sounds of its symbols were forgotten over the centuries, people have always kept knowing their meanings.
questions.d: Every language that people once wrote has died within a few years of losing its speakers, and hieroglyphics is the clearest example of this.
questions.explanation: The text says languages die *because* their native speakers and writers are gone, and that both the meanings and sounds of hieroglyphic symbols were lost over many centuries, which is exactly what B restates in reworded form. A reverses the cause and effect, C wrongly keeps the meanings known when the text says they were lost, and D changes "some" to "every" and "many centuries" to "a few years," so only B is truly synonymous.
